yaml.title: Alzheimer's Disease ML Classifier
yaml.subtitle: Identifying Hub Genes and Building a Machine Learning Classifier for Alzheimer's disease

markdown.markdown:
Alzheimer's disease remains the most common cause of dementia worldwide, yet diagnosis often occurs only after substantial neuronal damage has already developed.

This project investigates whether transcriptomic signatures can be leveraged to distinguish Alzheimer's disease from healthy ageing using machine learning.

Instead of relying solely on differential gene expression, the project integrates systems biology and network analysis to identify biologically meaningful molecular features before model construction.

The objective was not to determine whether computational models built from biologically relevant hub genes could successfully classify disease status

## Data & methods

- Street network intersection density from OpenStreetMap
- Land-use mix computed from parcel-level zoning data
- Transit stop proximity and pedestrian infrastructure data

The full scoring methodology and code are released openly so other cities can adapt it.

## Findings

Replace with a summary of validation results — how the index compares to existing walk-score products, and where it diverges and why.

## Notes

Placeholder text. Link to the GitHub repository, methodology write-up, and any partner cities using the index.

## Overview

Alzheimer's disease remains the most common cause of dementia worldwide, yet diagnosis often occurs only after substantial neuronal damage has already developed.

This project investigates whether transcriptomic signatures can be leveraged to distinguish Alzheimer's disease from healthy ageing using machine learning.

Instead of relying solely on differential gene expression, the project integrates systems biology and network analysis to identify biologically meaningful molecular features before model construction.

The objective was not to determine whether computational models built from biologically relevant hub genes could successfully classify disease status

## Data & methods

- Street network intersection density from OpenStreetMap
- Land-use mix computed from parcel-level zoning data
- Transit stop proximity and pedestrian infrastructure data

The full scoring methodology and code are released openly so other cities can adapt it.
